mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth acceleration (measured by GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter changes) drives positive equity valuations through improved corporate earnings expectations. When real GDP growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days are positive due to sustained demand expansion.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth rate exceeds 2.5% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:Latest GDPC1 vintage shows 23685.287 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2025-04-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 40% of instances when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains the primary discount rate anchor for equity valuations. When FEDFUNDS is below 3.5%, the low-rate regime supports higher equity multiples, producing positive 20-day forward returns as a baseline always-on signal independent of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 58% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 3.5%, or Rank IC is negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate increases of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signal deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity. This triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days as equity investors reprice earnings downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points in any 3-month rolling window
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC remains above -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both RSAFS grows month-over-month and HOUST exceeds 1.3 million annualized units, the combined demand signal produces positive 20-day SPX returns through improved corporate revenue expectations and construction-related economic activity.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth positive AND HOUST > 1.3 million annualized units
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 726283.0 million dollars, HOUST at 1321.0 thousand units as of 2025-06-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 45% of instances when both conditions are met, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real-side economic capacity utilization. When INDPRO declines more than 1.5% over a 3-month period, it signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice inflation and growth risks.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines more than 1.5% in any 3-month rolling window
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.9867 index points as of 2025-07-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC remains above -0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and monetary policy (FEDFUNDS) interact to determine real rates. When CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.5% while FEDFUNDS remains below 4.0%, real rates are negative, supporting equity valuations. This regime produces positive 5-day forward returns as investors seek real asset protection.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 322.132 index points, FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of 2025-07-01
regime_hint: negative real rates environment with inflation persistence
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 52% of triggered instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth signals labor market health and consumer income expansion. When PAYEMS grows month-over-month by more than 150,000 persons, it indicates sustained hiring momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive 60-day SPX forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159539 thousand persons as of 2025-07-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 45% of instances when PAYEMS growth exceeds 150,000, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline combined with retail sales (RSAFS) contraction signals demand destruction across both residential and consumer sectors. When both HOUST falls below 1.2 million annualized units and RSAFS shows month-over-month decline, negative 20-day SPX returns follow as investors anticipate earnings compression.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1.2 million annualized units AND RSAFS month-over-month growth negative
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1321.0 thousand units, RSAFS at 726283.0 million dollars as of 2025-06-01
regime_hint: demand recession or consumer retrenchment phase
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 2-year rolling window.
